The configuration file has syntax issues: inline comments using '#' after property values (e.g., 'spring.jpa.show-sql=true # Enable raw SQL logging') are not valid in Java properties files - the '#' and everything after it becomes part of the property value, which would cause 'true # Enable raw SQL logging' to be parsed as the value instead of just 'true', leading to boolean parsing errors.

# Server Configuration
server.port=8080
# Optional: Set a custom context path for the application
# server.servlet.context-path=/system-info

# MySQL Datasource Configuration
# Replace 'system_info_db' with your actual database name
spring.datasource.url=jdbc:mysql://localhost:3306/system_info_db?useSSL=false&serverTimezone=UTC&allowPublicKeyRetrieval=true
# Use environment variables in production to avoid hardcoding credentials
spring.datasource.username=${DB_USERNAME:root}
spring.datasource.password=${DB_PASSWORD:your_secure_password}
# Driver class for MySQL 8+ (compatible with Java 8+)
spring.datasource.driver-class-name=com.mysql.cj.jdbc.Driver

# HikariCP Connection Pool Settings (optimized for efficiency)
spring.datasource.hikari.maximum-pool-size=10
spring.datasource.hikari.minimum-idle=2
spring.datasource.hikari.idle-timeout=300000 # 5 minutes
spring.datasource.hikari.connection-timeout=20000 # 20 seconds

# JPA/Hibernate Configuration
# Use 'update' for development (auto-updates schema without dropping tables)
# In production, set to 'none' or 'validate' to prevent accidental schema changes
spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto=update
# Show SQL queries in logs (disable in production)
spring.jpa.show-sql=true
# Format SQL output for readability (disable in production)
spring.jpa.properties.hibernate.format_sql=true
# Hibernate dialect for MySQL 8+
spring.jpa.properties.hibernate.dialect=org.hibernate.dialect.MySQL8Dialect

# Production Security & Performance Recommendations:
# 1. Enable SSL: Change useSSL=false to useSSL=true in the datasource URL
# 2. Use a dedicated database user with minimal privileges (avoid root)
# 3. Store credentials in environment variables or a secure vault (never commit plaintext passwords)
# 4. Disable show-sql and format_sql to prevent sensitive data exposure in logs
# 5. Set spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto to 'none' or 'validate' to lock schema changes
# 6. Adjust Hikari pool sizes based on your production workload

# Server Configuration
# Override via environment variable SERVER_PORT if needed
server.port=${SERVER_PORT:8080}

# MySQL Database Connection Configuration
# Sensitive credentials are loaded from environment variables to avoid hardcoding secrets
spring.datasource.url=${DB_URL:jdbc:mysql://localhost:3306/system_info_db?useSSL=true&serverTimezone=UTC&useUnicode=true&characterEncoding=UTF-8&allowPublicKeyRetrieval=false}
spring.datasource.username=${DB_USERNAME:root}
spring.datasource.password=${DB_PASSWORD:}
spring.datasource.driver-class-name=com.mysql.cj.jdbc.Driver

# Hikari Connection Pool (Default in Spring Boot) - Efficiency & Resource Management
spring.datasource.hikari.maximum-pool-size=${DB_POOL_MAX:10}
spring.datasource.hikari.minimum-idle=${DB_POOL_MIN:2}
spring.datasource.hikari.idle-timeout=300000 # 5 minutes (close idle connections)
spring.datasource.hikari.connection-timeout=20000 # 20 seconds (timeout for connection acquisition)
spring.datasource.hikari.max-lifetime=1800000 # 30 minutes (max lifetime of connections)
spring.datasource.hikari.connection-test-query=SELECT 1 # Validate connections

# JPA/Hibernate Configuration (For Spring Data JPA Usage)
spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto=${JPA_DDL_AUTO:none} # Use 'none' in production; use 'create'/'update' only for development
spring.jpa.show-sql=${JPA_SHOW_SQL:false} # Disable in production to prevent sensitive data leakage
spring.jpa.properties.hibernate.dialect=org.hibernate.dialect.MySQL8Dialect
spring.jpa.properties.hibernate.format_sql=false
